message:
br-bd-9llzj FS_IOC_GETFSUUID ioctl: blkid-compatible fs UUID surface

Linux 6.5+ added FS_IOC_GETFSUUID (_IOR(0x15, 0, struct fsuuid2) =
0x80111500) so blkid, util-linux libuuid, and systemd can identify
a mount without reading the superblock directly. Pre-fix the FUSE
mount returned ENOTTY because dispatch_ioctl had no handler.

ffs-core surface:
- New `FsOps::fs_uuid() -> Result<[u8; 16]>` trait method (default
  returns UnsupportedFeature).
- OpenFs override surfaces ext4 sb.s_uuid for ext4 mounts and
  btrfs sb.fsid for btrfs mounts (the kernel reports btrfs's fsid
  as super_block::s_uuid).

ffs-fuse wiring:
- FS_IOC_GETFSUUID = 0x80111500 (computed from
  _IOR((dir=2)<<30 | (size=17)<<16 | (type=0x15)<<8 | nr=0)).
- dispatch_ioctl branch validates out_size >= 17 (else EINVAL),
  calls ops.fs_uuid(), encodes through encode_fsuuid_response.
- encode_fsuuid_response lays out struct fsuuid2 as: u8 len=16 +
  u8 uuid[16] (17 bytes total). debug_assert pins the size.
- IoctlRecordingFs gains a FsUuid trace + a deterministic 16-byte
  fixture so the encoder regression can pin every byte.

Tests: 690 ffs-core + 261 ffs-fuse pass. New regressions:
- ffs-fuse: encodes 17-byte struct with len=16 prefix + UUID body
  matching the IoctlRecordingFs fixture.
- ffs-fuse: short out_size (16) returns EINVAL.
- ffs-core: ext4_fs_uuid_returns_superblock_uuid asserts
  ops.fs_uuid() == sb.uuid for the live ext4 fixture and that the
  UUID is non-zero (sanity vs default).

fmt + clippy clean via rch CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cc_bd_9llzj.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
